description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a domed top and two prominent side outlets, each capped and secured with metal chains. The central body is cylindrical, featuring vertical ridges and a slightly flared base for stability. The top of the hydrant has a hexagonal operating nut, used for opening the valve. The surface is painted in a weathered red, showing signs of wear and some darkened areas, likely from exposure to the elements. The metal chains are attached to the side caps, preventing them from being lost when removed. No manufacturer or model markings are visible on the exterior.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with two side outlets, metal chains, and a domed top with a central operating nut.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main outlet, both side caps with attached chains, and the central operating nut on top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one outlet and its chain, with the cylindrical body and flared base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hydrant, both side outlets, and the absence of a front-facing outlet.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the other outlet and chain, with the domed top and base in profile.
